title: Mega Heracross Battle Guide
summary: A [Bug]/[Fighting] physical cannon that uses [skill-link] to lock every multi-hit move ([pin-missile], [bullet-seed], [rock-blast]) at a guaranteed 5 hits.
strengths: Base 185 Attack plus guaranteed 5-hit multi moves via [skill-link]
[pin-missile] and [rock-blast] shred [substitute], focus-sash, and [sturdy] in one turn
[close-combat] cleanly removes the [Dark] and [Steel] walls that normally wall [Bug] STAB
weaknesses: 4x [Flying] weakness — a [sing]le [brave-bird] can end the sweep
Base 75 Speed loses to nearly every unboosted meta [sweeper]

recommendedMoves.reason: Main [Bug] STAB — [skill-link] locks 5 hits every time
Top-tier [Fighting] STAB finisher that cracks [Dark] and [Steel] walls
Turns slow 75 Speed into a full team sweep via raw power

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Heracross jacks raw Attack from 125 to 185 and locks in [skill-link], the one ability that converts every multi-hit move into a guaranteed 5-strike sequence. In Champions MA, that gimmick is the entire reason to run it — no other [Bug]/[Fighting] cannon reliably deletes [substitute], focus-sash, or [sturdy] in a [sing]le turn.

## Strengths
[pin-missile] is base power 25 times 5 hits under [skill-link], which is an effective 125 BP before the [Bug] STAB, 185 Attack, and [adamant] boost stack on top. That number nukes any [Fairy], [Psychic], or [Dark] switch-in in a [sing]le turn. [rock-blast] is the meta-specific answer to [Flying] [revenge] killers — staraptor and talonflame both fall to the guaranteed 5-hit [Rock] spread. [close-combat] is the clean-up tool for Heatran and Excadrill lines that would otherwise hard-wall [Bug] damage, and once [swords-dance] goes up, the whole stack snowballs into a team sweep.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Bug]/[Fighting] is 4x weak to [Flying], and a [sing]le [brave-bird] or [hurricane] usually ends the sweep outright. Base 75 Speed also means Mega Heracross loses the Speed race to every 170-plus meta [sweeper] — the build must bring Speed control to compensate. 2x weaknesses to [Fire], [Psychic], [Fairy], and [Ghost] stack up fast, so direct matchups against Gengar, Tapu Lele, or Aegi[slash] should be avoided unless priority is set up behind it. [close-combat]'s Defense and Special Defense drops also make it fragile in drawn-out exchanges.

## Recommended Moveset
[pin-missile] is the locked main STAB — the moment [skill-link] is active, [substitute] and focus-sash both stop mattering. [close-combat] is the mandatory finisher against the [Steel] and [Dark] walls that ignore multi-hit [Bug] damage. [rock-blast] is picked over [rock-slide] specifically because [skill-link] removes its accuracy problems while keeping the anti-[Flying] punish. The last slot is [swords-dance] to convert the build from wallbreaker into full [sweeper]. If the team runs [trick-room], swap [swords-dance] for [megahorn] as a one-shot nuclear option.

## Item · Ability · Tera
The item is [heracronite], no alternatives. Skill-link is auto-locked on Mega Evolution, and it is the entire reason the build exists. Tera [Bug] maximizes [pin-missile] output and is the default, while Tera [Steel] is the tournament pick that removes the 4x [Flying] weakness and turns a lo[sing] [brave-bird] matchup into a survivable one.

## Teammates & Synergy
Priority users ([mach-punch], [aqua-jet], [bullet-punch]) paper over the base 75 Speed problem. Dedicated [Flying] answers ([Electric] or [Rock] types) must be on the roster to prevent the weakness from ruining the sweep. [stealth-rock] removal via a [defog] or [rapid-spin] partner is effectively mandatory, and a [trick-room] setter flips the Speed weakness into an advantage. [fake-out] support is the cleanest way to buy the one turn of [swords-dance].

## Play Patterns
Bring Mega Heracross in on turn 2 or later, not as the lead. Hold [close-combat] for the final sweep turn and push [pin-missile] and [rock-blast] first to avoid the Defense drops stacking. Tera the moment an oppo[sing] [Flying] attacker is telegraphed — that one c[lick] is the difference between a full sweep and lo[sing] the ace.
